behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources (metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, deployment log).
behaviors.justification: This is a pure orchestration and control-flow bug. The failure mode is 'does the code call all three sources and bundle their results before passing to summary?' Unit tests can mock all three sources to return fixed data, then verify that the orchestration layer correctly calls each and passes a complete bundle to the summary function. No external infrastructure is needed—the logic can be verified with controlled inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test that mocks all three data sources to return fixed test data. Call the orchestration/collection layer, capture the data bundle passed to the summary function, and assert that all three sources' results are present with correct structure. Verify no source is skipped and no data is dropped in transformation.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a 'data unavailable' note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Unit/Integration tests mocking exceptions would verify exception handling logic but would miss real failure modes: HTTP timeouts, connection drops, TCP resets, DNS failures, partial malformed responses, and server-side rate limiting. These failures appear differently than mocked 'exception' and require real network behavior or System-level test infrastructure (test server, network simulation) to validate graceful degradation.
behaviors.test_description: System test using a test HTTP server or network simulation to inject realistic unavailability for each data source. Simulate HTTP 5xx errors, connection timeouts, TCP resets, and DNS failures. Verify that report generation continues with data from available sources and includes 'data unavailable' notation for failed sections. Run multiple times to detect timing-dependent edge cases.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: If the 'narrative summary' is LLM-generated (typical for natural language synthesis), accuracy depends on real model behavior which is non-deterministic. Unit/Integration tests with mocked or template-based summaries would verify code paths but miss real LLM failures: hallucinations, semantic misrepresentation, value omission, and context drift. Real model inference is required to catch actual accuracy issues.
behaviors.test_description: Create test datasets with known key values (specific metrics, issue counts, dates). Call real summary generation with this data. Verify the output narrative contains the correct values without hallucination or omission. Check semantic accuracy (e.g., '42 bugs' not '42 deployments'). Run multiple times to detect non-determinism or consistency issues if LLM-based.

behaviors.description: A complete report—from data collection through email delivery—reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is 'does the email actually get delivered?' Mocking email delivery would verify code calls the email library, but miss real mail server failures: SMTP authentication, connection timeouts, server rejection, rate limiting, invalid headers, and delivery bounces. Real or test mail server infrastructure (smtp4dev, mailhog, etc.) is required to verify end-to-end delivery.
behaviors.test_description: System test with a test email server (smtp4dev, mailhog, or similar). Execute the complete end-to-end flow: collect data, generate summary, and send email. Verify the email arrives in the test server inbox with correct recipient, subject, body, and attachments. Check for malformed headers, SMTP errors, and authentication failures.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This requires testing real filesystem behavior (write failures, permissions, disk full, file readability) and actual scheduled execution (cron timing, job registration, retry triggering). Unit/Integration tests with mocked filesystem and scheduler would verify code logic but miss real failures: silent write failures, inaccessible backup directory, file locking, stale file handling, and the scheduler not actually invoking the retry. System-level testing with real I/O and job scheduling is needed.
behaviors.test_description: System test with real filesystem and scheduler. Simulate email delivery failure (SMTP unreachable). Verify: (1) report saves to backup file with correct content, (2) file is readable and permissions are correct, (3) on next scheduled run, retry logic executes and delivery is re-attempted. Monitor logs or metrics to confirm retry invocation. Test under constrained conditions (low disk space, restricted directory permissions).
